perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Receiving Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Receiving Party receives Confidential Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not 2 specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Interested Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Interested Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Interested Party receives Confidential Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not 2 specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Interested Party' in this section introduces ambiguity. The contract defines 'Receiving Party' in Section 1 but never defines 'Interested Party.' This makes it unclear whether the restrictions on soliciting employees apply to the Receiving Party, the Interested Party, or both, creating an in-text contradiction on the responsibilities and limits.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Interested Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Interested Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Interested Party receives Confidential Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not 2 specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Interested Party.' Since 'Interested Party' is not defined in the contract (as stated in the Perturbation Explanation), while 'Receiving Party' is defined in Section 1, the change introduces ambiguity regarding to whom the non-solicitation restrictions apply. This ambiguity creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which relies on a clear understanding of who the 'Receiving Party' is to enforce the non-solicitation clause. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, reinforcing the presence of the undefined 'Interested Party' within the same restrictive clause originally imposed on the 'Receiving Party', hence triggering Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Interested Organization will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Interested Organization or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Interested Organization' creates uncertainty in Section 7. The contract defines 'Receiving Party' in Section 1, implying that the return of confidential information is its responsibility. Introducing 'Interested Organization' without definition makes it ambiguous whether this new entity also has a duty to return information and/or if they are the same as the 'Receiving Party'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Interested Organization will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Interested Organization or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term 4 hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Confidential Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Confidential Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Confidential Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the 'Receiving Party' whose obligations are well-defined. The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Interested Organization' in Section 7. The Contradicted Text mentions both 'Interested Organization' and 'Receiving Party' in the same section. Because 'Interested Organization' is undefined and the change creates ambiguity about whether it has the same responsibilities as the 'Receiving Party', it introduces a contradiction. This creates an ambiguity as to who has to fulfill the confidentiality obligations as the contract now refers to two parties but only one is properly defined.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to):
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to the Involved Group pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Involved Group shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to):
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Receiving Party' with 'Involved Group' in the section defining the Standstill Agreement creates a contradiction. The text now implies the ‘Involved Group’ is subjected to the Standstill Agreement, while still refering to 'Receiving Party', generating confusion about who is subject to the Standstill restrictions
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to the Involved Group pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Involved Group shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to): (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of the Company, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company (including any derivative securities with economic equivalents of ownership of any of such securities), any right to vote or to direct the voting of any securities of the 3 Company or any assets of the Company, (b) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of the Company, (c) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, other than any group comprised solely of the Receiving Party and its affiliates, (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control, advise, change or influence the management, board of directors, governing instruments, policies or affairs of the Company, (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action that could require the Company to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement, other than the required amendment to the Receiving Party’s Schedule 13D filing as a result of the execution and delivery of this Agreement, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (g) have any discussions or enter into any arrangements (whether written or oral) with, or advise, assist or encourage any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Involved Group', while the subsequent text still refers to the 'Receiving Party' when describing the Standstill obligations. This creates a contradiction and confusion as to which party is bound by the Standstill Agreement as the Contradicted Text also uses this same conflicting language, indicating a strong in-text contradiction, per the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Concerned Organization and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Concerned Organization' in the amendment clause creates a potential contradiction in Section 14. The contract now implies that amendments require the signature of the 'Concerned Organization' when the rest of the contract uses defined terms like 'Receiving Party', and never defines the other, raising the question of whether an amendment signed only by the 'Receiving Party' is valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Concerned Organization and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Receiving Party' to 'Concerned Organization' creates a contradiction because the contract defines 'Receiving Party' but not 'Concerned Organization'. The original text specifies that amendments must be executed by the 'Receiving Party'. The changed text and contradicted text specify that amendments must be executed by 'Concerned Organization'. Since these terms are not used interchangeably or defined in relation to each other, it's unclear whether an amendment signed only by the 'Receiving Party' is valid, which is a strong contradiction based on the Misaligned Terminology definition, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Limitations on Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . (a) The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Limitations on Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . (a) The Examining Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Examining Party' here in Section 3(a) contradicts later sections by raising ambiguity about who the obligations extend to. The entire agreement defines the 'Receiving Party', including Section 7 regarding the return of confidential information and Section 6 regarding the standstill agreement. It is unclear now whether the term ‘Examining Party’ has the same obligations as the 'Receiving Party'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term 4 hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Confidential Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Confidential Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Confidential Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Receiving Party' to 'Examining Party' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text in Section 7 specifically references the 'Receiving Party' and their obligations regarding the return of confidential information. By introducing a new term, 'Examining Party,' without clarifying its relationship to 'Receiving Party,' it becomes unclear whether Section 7's obligations apply to the 'Examining Party'. This ambiguity directly contradicts the explicit terms of Section 7, creating a strong in-text contradiction, as it is uncertain who is bound by the obligation to return confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party is considering a possible business combination transaction (whether negotiated directly with the Company’s Board of Directors or otherwise) with respect to the Company (the “ Transaction ”), and, in order to assist the Receiving Party in evaluating the possible Transaction, the Company is prepared to make available to the Receiving Party certain information concerning the business, operations, strategy and prospects of the Company (all such information, the “ Confidential Information ”).
perturbation.changed_text: The Evaluating Entity is considering a possible business combination transaction (whether negotiated directly with the Company’s Board of Directors or otherwise) with respect to the Company (the “ Transaction ”), and, in order to assist the Evaluating Entity in evaluating the possible Transaction, the Company is prepared to make available to the Receiving Party certain information concerning the business, operations, strategy and prospects of the Company (all such information, the “ Confidential Information ”).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Evaluating Entity' introduces ambiguity in Section 1(a). The agreement then refers to the provision of information to the 'Receiving Party' which is not the evaluating entity, creating a contradiction. The text now creates an internal conflict over who is supposed to use the confidential information
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Evaluating Entity is considering a possible business combination transaction (whether negotiated directly with the Company’s Board of Directors or otherwise) with respect to the Company (the “ Transaction ”), and, in order to assist the Evaluating Entity in evaluating the possible Transaction, the Company is prepared to make available to the Receiving Party certain information concerning the business, operations, strategy and prospects of the Company (all such information, the “ Confidential Information ”). As a condition to the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party and the directors, officers, principals, partners, members, employees, agents, consultants, related investment funds, advisors, attorneys, accountants, affiliates, potential sources of capital and financing, and financial advisors (collectively, “ Representatives ”) of the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information strictly in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to otherwise comply, and to cause its Representatives to comply, with all obligations hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Receiving Party', but the changed text introduces 'Evaluating Entity' in the same context but then refers to providing information to the 'Receiving Party'. The 'Contradicted Text' repeats this contradiction. The change creates a contradiction because it is unclear who is to receive and use the confidential information, fulfilling the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' since the contract uses multiple terms ('Receiving Party' and 'Evaluating Entity') interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The explanation provided states 'The text now creates an internal conflict over who is supposed to use the confidential information'.
